meaning: n.警戒灯火管制,使电灯暗淡
examples: An emergency "brownout" was already in effect.
The more usual symptoms of brownout tend to be disengagement, discontent, and lethargy.
The causes of brownout are very much linked with the new world of work and changes that have taken place.
The cause was a rolling brownout, due to a California heat wave and excessive use of air conditioning.
Apparently, brownout was a factor in both (of) those (incidents).
